signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg -0.874% (n=7) with a G8 spread of 1.955%; the 290bp US-JGB yield gap favors USD, setting up a squeeze risk as JPY specs are short -88.5k. The next BoJ meeting is the catalyst for resolving the yield gap. JPY's 1W momentum creates a divergence with the US-JGB yield gap, introducing volatility risk.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA hold bias (0% priced for a hike) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The RBA's 4.10% rate with a hold bias drives the move. The next RBA meeting on 5 May determines whether the hold bias holds.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The tariff escalation reduces European export competitiveness. The next ECB meeting is likely to impact the pair's direction.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; BoE closed at 3.75% limits the upside — GBP COT long +32.6k sustains the bid. The BoE's 3.75% rate drives the move. The next BoE meeting determines whether the hold continues.

signals.title: USD/CAD — BoC Hold
signals.text: USD/CAD closed Friday at 1.3586; BoC closed at 2.25% sets up upside pressure — CAD COT short -54.7k reinforces the bias. The BoC's 2.25% rate drives the move. The next BoC meeting is the catalyst for resolving the hold.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, which sets up a short-squeeze risk as the RBNZ's forward bias points to a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This divergence creates risk of a sharp unwind, likely to pressure the NZD/USD lower. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is a key catalyst as the key catalyst.
